questions.qtype: decimal multiplication and division based on given product

questions.tex:
%(a)

Kat has a method for finding the difference between two square numbers, \(a^2 - b^2\). \\
Her method is

(the sum of \(a\) and \(b\)) \(\times\) (the difference between \(a\) and \(b\)).
  She shows her method for \(17^2 - 13^2\).

\fbox{
  \begin{minipage}{0.32\textwidth}
   \begin{align*}
    &17^2 - 13^2 \\
    &= (17 + 13) \times (17 - 13) \\
    &= 30 \times 4 \\
    &= 120\\
   \end{align*}
  \end{minipage}
 }
\medskip

  Work out \(29^2 - 21^2\) using Kat's method.\\[3cm]

Find the mass of carrots the grocer sells this week.

Find the total profit the grocer makes selling carrots this week.

Calculate the original price of 1 kg of potatoes.

(g) The grocer buys 620 kg of onions, correct to the nearest 20 kg.

Calculate the upper bound for the number of bags of onions that he packs.

The cost of each car is 1.48 euros. \\

He owns 57 cars, 12 bicycles and 42 trucks. \\

(a) The diagram shows a plan of Zak's garden.

Find the perimeter of the garden.

(b) Zak records the temperature in his garden each night for one week.

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|}

\end{tabular}

He buys 45 bean plants. \\

On Monday the barrel contains 120 litres of water. \\
On Friday the barrel contains 43.2 litres of water. \\
Calculate the percentage decrease of the water in the barrel.

The journey takes $1\frac{1}{4}$ hours. \\
He drives at an average speed of 57~km/h. \\
Calculate the distance he drives.

\begin{tabular}{|l|c|}
\hline

1st day               & \$22.60             \\ \hline
Each additional day   & \$11.80             \\ \hline
\end{tabular}
Calculate the number of days he hires it for.

Calculate the number of trees that remain.

(a) The table shows information about some of the planets in the solar system.

\begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|}
\hline
Planet & Diameter (km) & Average distance from the Sun (km) \\
\hline
Earth & 12 800 & $1.496 \times 10^8$ \\
Mars & 6 800 & $2.279 \times 10^8$ \\
Jupiter & 143 000 & $7.786 \times 10^8$ \\
Saturn & 120 500 & $1.434 \times 10^9$ \\
Neptune & 49 500 & $4.495 \times 10^9$ \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
(i) (b) The average distance of Mars from the Sun is $2.279 \times 10^8$ km.

Write this distance as an ordinary number.

(c) The planet Uranus has a diameter that is $35.8\%$ of the diameter of Jupiter.

Calculate the diameter of Uranus.

(d) The ratio diameter of Neptune : diameter of Saturn can be written in the form $1 : n$.

Find the value of $n$.

(e) Find the average distance of Neptune from the Sun as a percentage of the average distance of the Earth from the Sun.

(f) Distances within the solar system are also measured in astronomical units (AU).

The average distance of Jupiter from the Sun is 5.20 AU.

Calculate the average distance of Mars from the Sun in astronomical units.

(g) The diameter of Mars is $39.2\%$ greater than the diameter of Mercury.

Calculate the diameter of Mercury.

(h) One light year is the distance that light travels in a year of 365.25 days.

The speed of light is $2.9979 \times 10^5$ kilometres per second.

(i) (i) Show that one light year is $9.461 \times 10^{12}$ km, correct to 4 significant figures.

(j) The distance from the Andromeda Galaxy to Earth is $2.40 \times 10^{19}$ km.

Calculate the time taken for light to travel from this galaxy to Earth.

Give your answer in millions of years.

(a) Lucia has two fair spinners.

Spinner $A$ is five-sided and is numbered 1, 2, 3, 4, 5.

Spinner $B$ is nine-sided and is numbered 3, 3, 3, 4, 4, 4, 4, 5, 5.

Lucia spins the two spinners and records whether they land on a prime number.

(i) (b) Complete the tree diagram.

(c) Find the probability that

\begin{subsubparts}

(d) the two numbers are both prime,

(e) the two numbers are not both prime.

\end{subsubparts}

(f) Lucia spins Spinner $A$ 120 times.

Find the expected number of times the spinner lands on a prime number.

(g) Lucia spins Spinner $B$ twice.

Find the probability that the two numbers it lands on add up to 9 or more.

(h) Lucia keeps spinning Spinner $B$ until it lands on a 4.

Find an expression, in terms of $n$, for the probability that this happens on the $n$th spin.

Gretal paid \$290 more rent in 2019 than she did in 2018.

questions.qtype: compound interest, exponential growth, reverse exponential growth, finding time in exponential growth
questions.tex:

Calculate the value of this investment at the end of the 5 years.

(b) The value of a gold ring increases exponentially at a rate of 5\% per year.\\
The value is now \$882.

(i) (c) Calculate the value of the ring 2 years ago.

(d) Find the number of complete years it takes for the ring's value of \$882 to increase to a value greater than \$1100.

The cost of one protractor is $p$ cents.

The total cost of 5 rulers and 1 protractor is 245 cents.\\
The total cost of 2 rulers and 3 protractors is 215 cents.

Write down two equations in terms of $r$ and $p$ and solve these equations to find the cost of one protractor.

questions.qtype: Ratio simplification, total mass calculation, standard form
questions.tex:
\begin{question*}{1}{a}Mohsin has 600 pear trees and 720 apple trees on his farm.

 \begin{subpartsaliged}
 Write the ratio pear trees : apple trees in its simplest form.

 

 
 Each apple tree produces 16 boxes of apples each year.\\
 One box contains 18 kg of apples.

 Calculate the total mass of apples produced by the 720 trees in one year.\\
 Give your answer in standard form.

 

 \end{subpartsaliged}

 (b)
 \begin{subpartsaliged}
 One week, the total mass of pears picked was 18\,540 kg.\\
 For this week, the ratio mass of apples : mass of pears = $13 : 9$.

 Find the mass of apples picked that week.

 

 
 The apples cost Mohsin \$0.85 per kilogram to produce.\\
 He sells them at a profit of 60\%.

 Work out the selling price per kilogram of the apples.

 

 \end{subpartsaliged}

 (c)Mohsin exports some of his pears to a shop in Belgium.\\
 The shop buys the pears at \$1.50 per kilogram.\\
 The shop sells the pears for 2.30 euros per kilogram.\\
 The exchange rate is \$1 = 0.92 euros.

 Calculate the percentage profit per kilogram made by the shop.

 

 
 (d)Mohsin's earnings increase exponentially at a rate of 8.7\% each year.\\
 During 2018 he earned \$195\,600.

 During 2027, how much more does he earn than during 2018?

 

 
 \end{question*}

 Give your answer in kilometres per hour.

 


(b) Both cars start the race from the same position, $S$, at the same time.

(a) In a cycling club, the number of members are in the ratio males : females = 8 : 3.
 The club has 342 females.

(i) (b) Find the total number of members.

(c) Find the percentage of the total number of members that are female.

(d) The price of a bicycle is \$1020.
 Club members receive a 15\% discount on this price.

Find how much a club member pays for this bicycle.

(e) In 2019, the membership fee of the cycling club is \$79.50 .
 This is 6\% more than last year.

Find the increase in the cost of the membership.

(f) Asif cycles a distance of 105 km.
 On the first part of his journey he cycles 60 km in 2 hours 24 minutes.
 On the second part of his journey he cycles 45 km at 20 km/h.

Find his average speed for the whole journey.

(g) Bryan invested \$480 in an account 4 years ago.
 The account pays compound interest at a rate of 2.1\% per year.
 Today, he uses some of the money in this account to buy a bicycle costing \$430.

Calculate how much money remains in his account.

(h) The formula $s = \frac{1}{2}at^2$ is used to calculate the distance, $s$, travelled by a bicycle.
 When $a = 3$ and $t = 10$, each correct to the nearest integer, calculate the lower bound of the distance, $s$.

(a) A person from the group is chosen at random.

(b) Two people are chosen at random from the group of 200.

    Give your answer as a percentage.
